name: Ecommerce Store
professionalExpectations: An ecommerce store must make products, pricing, inventory, cart, checkout, fulfillment, support, and policies understandable.
Payment, tax, shipping, inventory, customer data, and production order handling are high-risk and gated by default.
The first version should prove catalog structure, product presentation, and buying flow without live payment processing.
Quality depends on trust, clarity, performance, and a controlled path to live commerce.
minimumQualityBar: Product, collection, pricing, cart, checkout-intent, order, and policy concepts are modeled before live payment exists.
No payment processor, tax service, shipping service, inventory system, production order data, or paid service is connected without approval.
Customer data handling, refund policy, fulfillment assumptions, backup, and rollback are documented.
Static catalog and non-live checkout intent can be reviewed locally before live commerce.
commonModules: Product catalog, collections, product detail, cart intent, checkout gate, order model, policies, and support information.
Inventory and fulfillment planning, pricing and tax assumptions, shipping rules, and return policy checklist.
Analytics and conversion planning without live tracking by default.
Future connector gates for payments, tax, shipping, inventory, email, and customer support.
uxExpectations: Product pages must show price, value, options, proof, delivery expectations, and support policies clearly.
Cart and checkout-intent flows must make blocked live payment status obvious until approved.
Navigation, filters, search, and mobile layout should be clean and fast.
Trust elements such as policies, contact path, and secure checkout expectations must be visible.

components: Static product catalog or local records first
Payment provider only after owner approval
Existing Postgres only after approved order and customer-data handling
Netlify only after approved staging or production deployment
recommendedStack.notes: Start with catalog and checkout-intent model. Do not process payments or orders until owner-approved.

phases.name: Catalog And Policies
goals: Define product, collection, pricing, policy, fulfillment, and support structure.
Map payment, customer-data, and paid-service approval gates.

phases.name: Storefront Prototype
goals: Build catalog, product detail, cart intent, and blocked checkout views locally.
Validate responsiveness, accessibility, and trust signals.

phases.name: Approved Commerce
goals: Add payments, orders, shipping, tax, and notifications only after approval and rollback planning.
Document monitoring, support, refund, and incident procedures.

knownPitfalls: Connecting payments before policies, product data, tax, and fulfillment are ready.
Treating checkout and order data as low-risk.
Overbuilding loyalty, recommendations, or advanced analytics before basic buying flow is trusted.
Using paid apps or storefront services without budget approval.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before payment, tax, shipping, inventory, email, SMS, analytics, deployment, domain, DNS, credentials, or paid tools.
Owner approval is required before handling customer data, production orders, refunds, or fulfillment records.
Owner approval is required before activating live checkout or collecting money.
Owner approval is required before connecting third-party storefront apps or paid extensions.
qualityChecklist: Catalog, product details, policies, cart intent, and checkout gate are clear.
No live payment, order, customer-data, deployment, or paid path exists without approval.
Mobile shopping flow, performance, accessibility, and trust signals are reviewed.
Backup, rollback, support, refund, and incident expectations are documented before live commerce.
whatToBuildFirst: Catalog data model and product page structure.
Static storefront with cart intent and blocked checkout state.
Policy, fulfillment, support, and trust sections.
Approval-gate map for payments, orders, and customer data.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Payment processing, tax automation, shipping labels, inventory sync, or live checkout.
Advanced recommendation engines, loyalty programs, or subscriptions.
Paid storefront apps before the basic catalog and buying flow are proven.
Production order management before backup and rollback planning.
stopConditions: Stop before payment, checkout activation, tax, shipping, inventory, customer data, production orders, credentials, paid tools, deployment, domain, or DNS changes.
Stop before collecting money or sending order-related messages.
Stop before connecting production databases or third-party commerce apps without approval.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not activate commerce, payments, orders, or storefront deployment.
